title: lonchocarpus lanceolatus subsp. lanceolatus
intro: Lonchocarpus lanceolatus subsp. lanceolatus is a plant species commonly known as the "Guacatonga" or "Coral Tree", which is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus lanceolatus subsp. lanceolatus include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are made by steeping the leaves, bark, or roots in hot water to release its medicinal properties.
